35#include <limits.h>

68PG_MODULE_MAGIC_EXT(
69					.name = "dblink",
70					.version = PG_VERSION
71);
72
73typedef struct remoteConn
74{
75	PGconn	   *conn;			/* Hold the remote connection */ view japanese
76	int			openCursorCount;	/* The number of open cursors */ view japanese
77	bool		newXactForCursor;	/* Opened a transaction for a cursor */ view japanese
78} remoteConn;
79
80typedef struct storeInfo
81{
82	FunctionCallInfo fcinfo;
83	Tuplestorestate *tuplestore;
84	AttInMetadata *attinmeta;
85	MemoryContext tmpcontext;
86	char	  **cstrs;
87	/* temp storage for results to avoid leaks on exception */ view japanese
88	PGresult   *last_res;
89	PGresult   *cur_res;
90} storeInfo;
91
92/*
93 * Internal declarations
94 */ view japanese
95static Datum dblink_record_internal(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, bool is_async);
96static void prepTuplestoreResult(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo);
97static void materializeResult(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, PGconn *conn,
98							  PGresult *res);
99static void materializeQueryResult(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo,
100								   PGconn *conn,
101								   const char *conname,
102								   const char *sql,
103								   bool fail);
104static PGresult *storeQueryResult(volatile storeInfo *sinfo, PGconn *conn, const char *sql);
105static void storeRow(volatile storeInfo *sinfo, PGresult *res, bool first);
106static remoteConn *getConnectionByName(const char *name);
107static HTAB *createConnHash(void);
108static remoteConn *createNewConnection(const char *name);
109static void deleteConnection(const char *name);
110static char **get_pkey_attnames(Relation rel, int16 *indnkeyatts);
111static char **get_text_array_contents(ArrayType *array, int *numitems);
112static char *get_sql_insert(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **src_pkattvals, char **tgt_pkattvals);
113static char *get_sql_delete(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **tgt_pkattvals);
114static char *get_sql_update(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **src_pkattvals, char **tgt_pkattvals);
115static char *quote_ident_cstr(char *rawstr);
116static int	get_attnum_pk_pos(int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, int key);
117static HeapTuple get_tuple_of_interest(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **src_pkattvals);
118static Relation get_rel_from_relname(text *relname_text, LOCKMODE lockmode, AclMode aclmode);
119static char *generate_relation_name(Relation rel);
120static void dblink_connstr_check(const char *connstr);
121static bool dblink_connstr_has_pw(const char *connstr);
122static void dblink_security_check(PGconn *conn, const char *connname,
123								  const char *connstr);
124static void dblink_res_error(PGconn *conn, const char *conname, PGresult *res,
125							 bool fail, const char *fmt,...) pg_attribute_printf(5, 6);
126static char *get_connect_string(const char *servername);
127static char *escape_param_str(const char *str);
128static void validate_pkattnums(Relation rel,
129							   int2vector *pkattnums_arg, int32 pknumatts_arg,
130							   int **pkattnums, int *pknumatts);
131static bool is_valid_dblink_option(const PQconninfoOption *options,
132								   const char *option, Oid context);
133static int	applyRemoteGucs(PGconn *conn);
134static void restoreLocalGucs(int nestlevel);
135static bool UseScramPassthrough(ForeignServer *foreign_server, UserMapping *user);
136static void appendSCRAMKeysInfo(StringInfo buf);
137static bool is_valid_dblink_fdw_option(const PQconninfoOption *options, const char *option,
138									   Oid context);
139static bool dblink_connstr_has_required_scram_options(const char *connstr);
140
141/* Global */ view japanese
142static remoteConn *pconn = NULL;
143static HTAB *remoteConnHash = NULL;
144
145/* custom wait event values, retrieved from shared memory */ view japanese
146static uint32 dblink_we_connect = 0;
147static uint32 dblink_we_get_conn = 0;
148static uint32 dblink_we_get_result = 0;
149
150/*
151 *	Following is hash that holds multiple remote connections.
152 *	Calling convention of each dblink function changes to accept
153 *	connection name as the first parameter. The connection hash is
154 *	much like ecpg e.g. a mapping between a name and a PGconn object.
155 *
156 *	To avoid potentially leaking a PGconn object in case of out-of-memory
157 *	errors, we first create the hash entry, then open the PGconn.
158 *	Hence, a hash entry whose rconn.conn pointer is NULL must be
159 *	understood as a leftover from a failed create; it should be ignored
160 *	by lookup operations, and silently replaced by create operations.
161 */ view japanese
162
163typedef struct remoteConnHashEnt
164{
165	char		name[NAMEDATALEN];
166	remoteConn	rconn;
167} remoteConnHashEnt;
168
169/* initial number of connection hashes */ view japanese
170#define NUMCONN 16
171
172static char *
173xpstrdup(const char *in)
174{
175	if (in == NULL)
176		return NULL;
177	return pstrdup(in);
178}
179
180pg_noreturn static void
181dblink_res_internalerror(PGconn *conn, PGresult *res, const char *p2)
182{
183	char	   *msg = pchomp(PQerrorMessage(conn));
184
185	PQclear(res);
186	elog(ERROR, "%s: %s", p2, msg);
187}
188
189pg_noreturn static void
190dblink_conn_not_avail(const char *conname)
191{
192	if (conname)
193		ereport(ERROR,
194				(errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_DOES_NOT_EXIST),
195				 errmsg("connection \"%s\" not available", conname)));
196	else
197		ereport(ERROR,
198				(errcode(ERRCODE_CONNECTION_DOES_NOT_EXIST),
199				 errmsg("connection not available")));
200}
201
202static void
203dblink_get_conn(char *conname_or_str,
204				PGconn *volatile *conn_p, char **conname_p, volatile bool *freeconn_p)
205{
206	remoteConn *rconn = getConnectionByName(conname_or_str);
207	PGconn	   *conn;
208	char	   *conname;
209	bool		freeconn;
210
211	if (rconn)
212	{
213		conn = rconn->conn;
214		conname = conname_or_str;
215		freeconn = false;
216	}
217	else
218	{
219		const char *connstr;
220
221		connstr = get_connect_string(conname_or_str);
222		if (connstr == NULL)
223			connstr = conname_or_str;
224		dblink_connstr_check(connstr);
225
226		/* first time, allocate or get the custom wait event */
227		if (dblink_we_get_conn == 0)
228			dblink_we_get_conn = WaitEventExtensionNew("DblinkGetConnect");
229
230		/* OK to make connection */
231		conn = libpqsrv_connect(connstr, dblink_we_get_conn);
232
233		if (PQstatus(conn) == CONNECTION_BAD)
234		{
235			char	   *msg = pchomp(PQerrorMessage(conn));
236
237			libpqsrv_disconnect(conn);
238			ereport(ERROR,
239					(errcode(ERRCODE_SQLCLIENT_UNABLE_TO_ESTABLISH_SQLCONNECTION),
240					 errmsg("could not establish connection"),
241					 errdetail_internal("%s", msg)));
242		}
243		dblink_security_check(conn, NULL, connstr);
244		if (PQclientEncoding(conn) != GetDatabaseEncoding())
245			PQsetClientEncoding(conn, GetDatabaseEncodingName());
246		freeconn = true;
247		conname = NULL;
248	}
249
250	*conn_p = conn;
251	*conname_p = conname;
252	*freeconn_p = freeconn;
253}
254
255static PGconn *
256dblink_get_named_conn(const char *conname)
257{
258	remoteConn *rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
259
260	if (rconn)
261		return rconn->conn;
262
263	dblink_conn_not_avail(conname);
264	return NULL;				/* keep compiler quiet */ view japanese
265}
266
267static void
268dblink_init(void)
269{
270	if (!pconn)
271	{
272		if (dblink_we_get_result == 0)
273			dblink_we_get_result = WaitEventExtensionNew("DblinkGetResult");
274
275		pconn = (remoteConn *) MemoryContextAlloc(TopMemoryContext, sizeof(remoteConn));
276		pconn->conn = NULL;
277		pconn->openCursorCount = 0;
278		pconn->newXactForCursor = false;
279	}
280}
281
282/*
283 * Create a persistent connection to another database
284 */ view japanese
285PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_connect);
286Datum
287dblink_connect(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
288{
289	char	   *conname_or_str = NULL;
290	char	   *connstr = NULL;
291	char	   *connname = NULL;
292	char	   *msg;
293	PGconn	   *conn = NULL;
294	remoteConn *rconn = NULL;
295
296	dblink_init();
297
298	if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
299	{
300		conname_or_str = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
301		connname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
302	}
303	else if (PG_NARGS() == 1)
304		conname_or_str = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
305
306	/* first check for valid foreign data server */ view japanese
307	connstr = get_connect_string(conname_or_str);
308	if (connstr == NULL)
309		connstr = conname_or_str;
310
311	/* check password in connection string if not superuser */ view japanese
312	dblink_connstr_check(connstr);
313
314	/* first time, allocate or get the custom wait event */ view japanese
315	if (dblink_we_connect == 0)
316		dblink_we_connect = WaitEventExtensionNew("DblinkConnect");
317
318	/* if we need a hashtable entry, make that first, since it might fail */ view japanese
319	if (connname)
320	{
321		rconn = createNewConnection(connname);
322		Assert(rconn->conn == NULL);
323	}
324
325	/* OK to make connection */ view japanese
326	conn = libpqsrv_connect(connstr, dblink_we_connect);
327
328	if (PQstatus(conn) == CONNECTION_BAD)
329	{
330		msg = pchomp(PQerrorMessage(conn));
331		libpqsrv_disconnect(conn);
332		if (connname)
333			deleteConnection(connname);
334
335		ereport(ERROR,
336				(errcode(ERRCODE_SQLCLIENT_UNABLE_TO_ESTABLISH_SQLCONNECTION),
337				 errmsg("could not establish connection"),
338				 errdetail_internal("%s", msg)));
339	}
340
341	/* check password actually used if not superuser */ view japanese
342	dblink_security_check(conn, connname, connstr);
343
344	/* attempt to set client encoding to match server encoding, if needed */ view japanese
345	if (PQclientEncoding(conn) != GetDatabaseEncoding())
346		PQsetClientEncoding(conn, GetDatabaseEncodingName());
347
348	/* all OK, save away the conn */ view japanese
349	if (connname)
350	{
351		rconn->conn = conn;
352	}
353	else
354	{
355		if (pconn->conn)
356			libpqsrv_disconnect(pconn->conn);
357		pconn->conn = conn;
358	}
359
360	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text("OK"));
361}
362
363/*
364 * Clear a persistent connection to another database
365 */ view japanese
366PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_disconnect);
367Datum
368dblink_disconnect(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
369{
370	char	   *conname = NULL;
371	remoteConn *rconn = NULL;
372	PGconn	   *conn = NULL;
373
374	dblink_init();
375
376	if (PG_NARGS() == 1)
377	{
378		conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
379		rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
380		if (rconn)
381			conn = rconn->conn;
382	}
383	else
384		conn = pconn->conn;
385
386	if (!conn)
387		dblink_conn_not_avail(conname);
388
389	libpqsrv_disconnect(conn);
390	if (rconn)
391		deleteConnection(conname);
392	else
393		pconn->conn = NULL;
394
395	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text("OK"));
396}
397
398/*
399 * opens a cursor using a persistent connection
400 */ view japanese
401PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_open);
402Datum
403dblink_open(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
404{
405	PGresult   *res = NULL;
406	PGconn	   *conn;
407	char	   *curname = NULL;
408	char	   *sql = NULL;
409	char	   *conname = NULL;
410	StringInfoData buf;
411	remoteConn *rconn = NULL;
412	bool		fail = true;	/* default to backward compatible behavior */
413
414	dblink_init();
415	initStringInfo(&buf);
416
417	if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
418	{
419		/* text,text */ view japanese
420		curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
421		sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
422		rconn = pconn;
423	}
424	else if (PG_NARGS() == 3)
425	{
426		/* might be text,text,text or text,text,bool */ view japanese
427		if (get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 2) == BOOLOID)
428		{
429			curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
430			sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
431			fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(2);
432			rconn = pconn;
433		}
434		else
435		{
436			conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
437			curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
438			sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(2));
439			rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
440		}
441	}
442	else if (PG_NARGS() == 4)
443	{
444		/* text,text,text,bool */ view japanese
445		conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
446		curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
447		sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(2));
448		fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(3);
449		rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
450	}
451
452	if (!rconn || !rconn->conn)
453		dblink_conn_not_avail(conname);
454
455	conn = rconn->conn;
456
457	/* If we are not in a transaction, start one */ view japanese
458	if (PQtransactionStatus(conn) == PQTRANS_IDLE)
459	{
460		res = libpqsrv_exec(conn, "BEGIN", dblink_we_get_result);
461		if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
462			dblink_res_internalerror(conn, res, "begin error");
463		PQclear(res);
464		rconn->newXactForCursor = true;
465
466		/*
467		 * Since transaction state was IDLE, we force cursor count to
468		 * initially be 0. This is needed as a previous ABORT might have wiped
469		 * out our transaction without maintaining the cursor count for us.
470		 */ view japanese
471		rconn->openCursorCount = 0;
472	}
473
474	/* if we started a transaction, increment cursor count */ view japanese
475	if (rconn->newXactForCursor)
476		(rconn->openCursorCount)++;
477
478	appendStringInfo(&buf, "DECLARE %s CURSOR FOR %s", curname, sql);
479	res = libpqsrv_exec(conn, buf.data, dblink_we_get_result);
480	if (!res || PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
481	{
482		dblink_res_error(conn, conname, res, fail,
483						 "while opening cursor \"%s\"", curname);
484		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text("ERROR"));
485	}
486
487	PQclear(res);
488	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text("OK"));
489}
490
491/*
492 * closes a cursor
493 */ view japanese
494PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_close);
495Datum
496dblink_close(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
497{
498	PGconn	   *conn;
499	PGresult   *res = NULL;
500	char	   *curname = NULL;
501	char	   *conname = NULL;
502	StringInfoData buf;
503	remoteConn *rconn = NULL;
504	bool		fail = true;	/* default to backward compatible behavior */
505
506	dblink_init();
507	initStringInfo(&buf);
508
509	if (PG_NARGS() == 1)
510	{
511		/* text */
512		curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
513		rconn = pconn;
514	}
515	else if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
516	{
517		/* might be text,text or text,bool */
518		if (get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 1) == BOOLOID)
519		{
520			curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
521			fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(1);
522			rconn = pconn;
523		}
524		else
525		{
526			conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
527			curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
528			rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
529		}
530	}
531	if (PG_NARGS() == 3)
532	{
533		/* text,text,bool */
534		conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
535		curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
536		fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(2);
537		rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
538	}
539
540	if (!rconn || !rconn->conn)
541		dblink_conn_not_avail(conname);
542
543	conn = rconn->conn;
544
545	appendStringInfo(&buf, "CLOSE %s", curname);
546
547	/* close the cursor */ view japanese
548	res = libpqsrv_exec(conn, buf.data, dblink_we_get_result);
549	if (!res || PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
550	{
551		dblink_res_error(conn, conname, res, fail,
552						 "while closing cursor \"%s\"", curname);
553		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text("ERROR"));
554	}
555
556	PQclear(res);
557
558	/* if we started a transaction, decrement cursor count */ view japanese
559	if (rconn->newXactForCursor)
560	{
561		(rconn->openCursorCount)--;
562
563		/* if count is zero, commit the transaction */ view japanese
564		if (rconn->openCursorCount == 0)
565		{
566			rconn->newXactForCursor = false;
567
568			res = libpqsrv_exec(conn, "COMMIT", dblink_we_get_result);
569			if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
570				dblink_res_internalerror(conn, res, "commit error");
571			PQclear(res);
572		}
573	}
574
575	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text("OK"));
576}
577
578/*
579 * Fetch results from an open cursor
580 */ view japanese
581PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_fetch);
582Datum
583dblink_fetch(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
584{
585	PGresult   *res = NULL;
586	char	   *conname = NULL;
587	remoteConn *rconn = NULL;
588	PGconn	   *conn = NULL;
589	StringInfoData buf;
590	char	   *curname = NULL;
591	int			howmany = 0;
592	bool		fail = true;	/* default to backward compatible */
593
594	prepTuplestoreResult(fcinfo);
595
596	dblink_init();
597
598	if (PG_NARGS() == 4)
599	{
600		/* text,text,int,bool */ view japanese
601		conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
602		curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
603		howmany = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
604		fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(3);
605
606		rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
607		if (rconn)
608			conn = rconn->conn;
609	}
610	else if (PG_NARGS() == 3)
611	{
612		/* text,text,int or text,int,bool */ view japanese
613		if (get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 2) == BOOLOID)
614		{
615			curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
616			howmany = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
617			fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(2);
618			conn = pconn->conn;
619		}
620		else
621		{
622			conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
623			curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
624			howmany = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
625
626			rconn = getConnectionByName(conname);
627			if (rconn)
628				conn = rconn->conn;
629		}
630	}
631	else if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
632	{
633		/* text,int */ view japanese
634		curname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
635		howmany = PG_GETARG_INT32(1);
636		conn = pconn->conn;
637	}
638
639	if (!conn)
640		dblink_conn_not_avail(conname);
641
642	initStringInfo(&buf);
643	appendStringInfo(&buf, "FETCH %d FROM %s", howmany, curname);
644
645	/*
646	 * Try to execute the query.  Note that since libpq uses malloc, the
647	 * PGresult will be long-lived even though we are still in a short-lived
648	 * memory context.
649	 */ view japanese
650	res = libpqsrv_exec(conn, buf.data, dblink_we_get_result);
651	if (!res ||
652		(PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK &&
653		 PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK))
654	{
655		dblink_res_error(conn, conname, res, fail,
656						 "while fetching from cursor \"%s\"", curname);
657		return (Datum) 0;
658	}
659	else if (PQresultStatus(res) == PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
660	{
661		/* cursor does not exist - closed already or bad name */ view japanese
662		PQclear(res);
663		ereport(ERROR,
664				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_CURSOR_NAME),
665				 errmsg("cursor \"%s\" does not exist", curname)));
666	}
667
668	materializeResult(fcinfo, conn, res);
669	return (Datum) 0;
670}
671
672/*
673 * Note: this is the new preferred version of dblink
674 */ view japanese
675PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_record);
676Datum
677dblink_record(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
678{
679	return dblink_record_internal(fcinfo, false);
680}
681
682PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_send_query);
683Datum
684dblink_send_query(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
685{
686	PGconn	   *conn;
687	char	   *sql;
688	int			retval;
689
690	if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
691	{
692		conn = dblink_get_named_conn(text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0)));
693		sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
694	}
695	else
696		/* shouldn't happen */
697		elog(ERROR, "wrong number of arguments");
698
699	/* async query send */ view japanese
700	retval = PQsendQuery(conn, sql);
701	if (retval != 1)
702		elog(NOTICE, "could not send query: %s", pchomp(PQerrorMessage(conn)));
703
704	PG_RETURN_INT32(retval);
705}
706
707PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_get_result);
708Datum
709dblink_get_result(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
710{
711	return dblink_record_internal(fcinfo, true);
712}
713
714static Datum
715dblink_record_internal(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, bool is_async)
716{
717	PGconn	   *volatile conn = NULL;
718	volatile bool freeconn = false;
719
720	prepTuplestoreResult(fcinfo);
721
722	dblink_init();
723
724	PG_TRY();
725	{
726		char	   *sql = NULL;
727		char	   *conname = NULL;
728		bool		fail = true;	/* default to backward compatible */ view japanese
729
730		if (!is_async)
731		{
732			if (PG_NARGS() == 3)
733			{
734				/* text,text,bool */ view japanese
735				conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
736				sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
737				fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(2);
738				dblink_get_conn(conname, &conn, &conname, &freeconn);
739			}
740			else if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
741			{
742				/* text,text or text,bool */ view japanese
743				if (get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 1) == BOOLOID)
744				{
745					sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
746					fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(1);
747					conn = pconn->conn;
748				}
749				else
750				{
751					conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
752					sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
753					dblink_get_conn(conname, &conn, &conname, &freeconn);
754				}
755			}
756			else if (PG_NARGS() == 1)
757			{
758				/* text */
759				conn = pconn->conn;
760				sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
761			}
762			else
763				/* shouldn't happen */
764				elog(ERROR, "wrong number of arguments");
765		}
766		else					/* is_async */ view japanese
767		{
768			/* get async result */ view japanese
769			conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
770
771			if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
772			{
773				/* text,bool */ view japanese
774				fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(1);
775				conn = dblink_get_named_conn(conname);
776			}
777			else if (PG_NARGS() == 1)
778			{
779				/* text */ view japanese
780				conn = dblink_get_named_conn(conname);
781			}
782			else
783				/* shouldn't happen */
784				elog(ERROR, "wrong number of arguments");
785		}
786
787		if (!conn)
788			dblink_conn_not_avail(conname);
789
790		if (!is_async)
791		{
792			/* synchronous query, use efficient tuple collection method */ view japanese
793			materializeQueryResult(fcinfo, conn, conname, sql, fail);
794		}
795		else
796		{
797			/* async result retrieval, do it the old way */ view japanese
798			PGresult   *res = libpqsrv_get_result(conn, dblink_we_get_result);
799
800			/* NULL means we're all done with the async results */ view japanese
801			if (res)
802			{
803				if (PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK &&
804					PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
805				{
806					dblink_res_error(conn, conname, res, fail,
807									 "while executing query");
808					/* if fail isn't set, we'll return an empty query result */
809				}
810				else
811				{
812					materializeResult(fcinfo, conn, res);
813				}
814			}
815		}
816	}
817	PG_FINALLY();
818	{
819		/* if needed, close the connection to the database */
820		if (freeconn)
821			libpqsrv_disconnect(conn);
822	}
823	PG_END_TRY();
824
825	return (Datum) 0;
826}
827
828/*
829 * Verify function caller can handle a tuplestore result, and set up for that.
830 *
831 * Note: if the caller returns without actually creating a tuplestore, the
832 * executor will treat the function result as an empty set.
833 */ view japanese
834static void
835prepTuplestoreResult(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo)
836{
837	ReturnSetInfo *rsinfo = (ReturnSetInfo *) fcinfo->resultinfo;
838
839	/* check to see if query supports us returning a tuplestore */ view japanese
840	if (rsinfo == NULL || !IsA(rsinfo, ReturnSetInfo))
841		ereport(ERROR,
842				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
843				 errmsg("set-valued function called in context that cannot accept a set")));
844	if (!(rsinfo->allowedModes & SFRM_Materialize))
845		ereport(ERROR,
846				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
847				 errmsg("materialize mode required, but it is not allowed in this context")));
848
849	/* let the executor know we're sending back a tuplestore */ view japanese
850	rsinfo->returnMode = SFRM_Materialize;
851
852	/* caller must fill these to return a non-empty result */ view japanese
853	rsinfo->setResult = NULL;
854	rsinfo->setDesc = NULL;
855}
856
857/*
858 * Copy the contents of the PGresult into a tuplestore to be returned
859 * as the result of the current function.
860 * The PGresult will be released in this function.
861 */ view japanese
862static void
863materializeResult(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo, PGconn *conn, PGresult *res)
864{
865	ReturnSetInfo *rsinfo = (ReturnSetInfo *) fcinfo->resultinfo;
866
867	/* prepTuplestoreResult must have been called previously */
868	Assert(rsinfo->returnMode == SFRM_Materialize);
869
870	PG_TRY();
871	{
872		TupleDesc	tupdesc;
873		bool		is_sql_cmd;
874		int			ntuples;
875		int			nfields;
876
877		if (PQresultStatus(res) == PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
878		{
879			is_sql_cmd = true;
880
881			/*
882			 * need a tuple descriptor representing one TEXT column to return
883			 * the command status string as our result tuple
884			 */
885			tupdesc = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(1);
886			TupleDescInitEntry(tupdesc, (AttrNumber) 1, "status",
887							   TEXTOID, -1, 0);
888			ntuples = 1;
889			nfields = 1;
890		}
891		else
892		{
893			Assert(PQresultStatus(res) == PGRES_TUPLES_OK);
894
895			is_sql_cmd = false;
896
897			/* get a tuple descriptor for our result type */
898			switch (get_call_result_type(fcinfo, NULL, &tupdesc))
899			{
900				case TYPEFUNC_COMPOSITE:
901					/* success */
902					break;
903				case TYPEFUNC_RECORD:
904					/* failed to determine actual type of RECORD */
905					ereport(ERROR,
906							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
907							 errmsg("function returning record called in context "
908									"that cannot accept type record")));
909					break;
910				default:
911					/* result type isn't composite */
912					elog(ERROR, "return type must be a row type");
913					break;
914			}
915
916			/* make sure we have a persistent copy of the tupdesc */
917			tupdesc = CreateTupleDescCopy(tupdesc);
918			ntuples = PQntuples(res);
919			nfields = PQnfields(res);
920		}
921
922		/*
923		 * check result and tuple descriptor have the same number of columns
924		 */ view japanese
925		if (nfields != tupdesc->natts)
926			ereport(ERROR,
927					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
928					 errmsg("remote query result rowtype does not match "
929							"the specified FROM clause rowtype")));
930
931		if (ntuples > 0)
932		{
933			AttInMetadata *attinmeta;
934			int			nestlevel = -1;
935			Tuplestorestate *tupstore;
936			MemoryContext oldcontext;
937			int			row;
938			char	  **values;
939
940			attinmeta = TupleDescGetAttInMetadata(tupdesc);
941
942			/* Set GUCs to ensure we read GUC-sensitive data types correctly */ view japanese
943			if (!is_sql_cmd)
944				nestlevel = applyRemoteGucs(conn);
945
946			oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(rsinfo->econtext->ecxt_per_query_memory);
947			tupstore = tuplestore_begin_heap(true, false, work_mem);
948			rsinfo->setResult = tupstore;
949			rsinfo->setDesc = tupdesc;
950			MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
951
952			values = palloc_array(char *, nfields);
953
954			/* put all tuples into the tuplestore */ view japanese
955			for (row = 0; row < ntuples; row++)
956			{
957				HeapTuple	tuple;
958
959				if (!is_sql_cmd)
960				{
961					int			i;
962
963					for (i = 0; i < nfields; i++)
964					{
965						if (PQgetisnull(res, row, i))
966							values[i] = NULL;
967						else
968							values[i] = PQgetvalue(res, row, i);
969					}
970				}
971				else
972				{
973					values[0] = PQcmdStatus(res);
974				}
975
976				/* build the tuple and put it into the tuplestore. */
977				tuple = BuildTupleFromCStrings(attinmeta, values);
978				tuplestore_puttuple(tupstore, tuple);
979			}
980
981			/* clean up GUC settings, if we changed any */
982			restoreLocalGucs(nestlevel);
983		}
984	}
985	PG_FINALLY();
986	{
987		/* be sure to release the libpq result */ view japanese
988		PQclear(res);
989	}
990	PG_END_TRY();
991}
992
993/*
994 * Execute the given SQL command and store its results into a tuplestore
995 * to be returned as the result of the current function.
996 *
997 * This is equivalent to PQexec followed by materializeResult, but we make
998 * use of libpq's single-row mode to avoid accumulating the whole result
999 * inside libpq before it gets transferred to the tuplestore.
1000 */ view japanese
1001static void
1002materializeQueryResult(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo,
1003					   PGconn *conn,
1004					   const char *conname,
1005					   const char *sql,
1006					   bool fail)
1007{
1008	ReturnSetInfo *rsinfo = (ReturnSetInfo *) fcinfo->resultinfo;
1009	PGresult   *volatile res = NULL;
1010	volatile storeInfo sinfo = {0};
1011
1012	/* prepTuplestoreResult must have been called previously */ view japanese
1013	Assert(rsinfo->returnMode == SFRM_Materialize);
1014
1015	sinfo.fcinfo = fcinfo;
1016
1017	PG_TRY();
1018	{
1019		/* Create short-lived memory context for data conversions */ view japanese
1020		sinfo.tmpcontext = AllocSetContextCreate(CurrentMemoryContext,
1021												 "dblink temporary context",
1022												 ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
1023
1024		/* execute query, collecting any tuples into the tuplestore */ view japanese
1025		res = storeQueryResult(&sinfo, conn, sql);
1026
1027		if (!res ||
1028			(PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK &&
1029			 PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK))
1030		{
1031			/*
1032			 * dblink_res_error will clear the passed PGresult, so we need
1033			 * this ugly dance to avoid doing so twice during error exit
1034			 */ view japanese
1035			PGresult   *res1 = res;
1036
1037			res = NULL;
1038			dblink_res_error(conn, conname, res1, fail,
1039							 "while executing query");
1040			/* if fail isn't set, we'll return an empty query result */ view japanese
1041		}
1042		else if (PQresultStatus(res) == PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
1043		{
1044			/*
1045			 * storeRow didn't get called, so we need to convert the command
1046			 * status string to a tuple manually
1047			 */ view japanese
1048			TupleDesc	tupdesc;
1049			AttInMetadata *attinmeta;
1050			Tuplestorestate *tupstore;
1051			HeapTuple	tuple;
1052			char	   *values[1];
1053			MemoryContext oldcontext;
1054
1055			/*
1056			 * need a tuple descriptor representing one TEXT column to return
1057			 * the command status string as our result tuple
1058			 */ view japanese
1059			tupdesc = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(1);
1060			TupleDescInitEntry(tupdesc, (AttrNumber) 1, "status",
1061							   TEXTOID, -1, 0);
1062			attinmeta = TupleDescGetAttInMetadata(tupdesc);
1063
1064			oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(rsinfo->econtext->ecxt_per_query_memory);
1065			tupstore = tuplestore_begin_heap(true, false, work_mem);
1066			rsinfo->setResult = tupstore;
1067			rsinfo->setDesc = tupdesc;
1068			MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
1069
1070			values[0] = PQcmdStatus(res);
1071
1072			/* build the tuple and put it into the tuplestore. */ view japanese
1073			tuple = BuildTupleFromCStrings(attinmeta, values);
1074			tuplestore_puttuple(tupstore, tuple);
1075
1076			PQclear(res);
1077			res = NULL;
1078		}
1079		else
1080		{
1081			Assert(PQresultStatus(res) == PGRES_TUPLES_OK);
1082			/* storeRow should have created a tuplestore */ view japanese
1083			Assert(rsinfo->setResult != NULL);
1084
1085			PQclear(res);
1086			res = NULL;
1087		}
1088
1089		/* clean up data conversion short-lived memory context */ view japanese
1090		if (sinfo.tmpcontext != NULL)
1091			MemoryContextDelete(sinfo.tmpcontext);
1092		sinfo.tmpcontext = NULL;
1093
1094		PQclear(sinfo.last_res);
1095		sinfo.last_res = NULL;
1096		PQclear(sinfo.cur_res);
1097		sinfo.cur_res = NULL;
1098	}
1099	PG_CATCH();
1100	{
1101		/* be sure to release any libpq result we collected */ view japanese
1102		PQclear(res);
1103		PQclear(sinfo.last_res);
1104		PQclear(sinfo.cur_res);
1105		/* and clear out any pending data in libpq */ view japanese
1106		while ((res = libpqsrv_get_result(conn, dblink_we_get_result)) !=
1107			   NULL)
1108			PQclear(res);
1109		PG_RE_THROW();
1110	}
1111	PG_END_TRY();
1112}
1113
1114/*
1115 * Execute query, and send any result rows to sinfo->tuplestore.
1116 */ view japanese
1117static PGresult *
1118storeQueryResult(volatile storeInfo *sinfo, PGconn *conn, const char *sql)
1119{
1120	bool		first = true;
1121	int			nestlevel = -1;
1122	PGresult   *res;
1123
1124	if (!PQsendQuery(conn, sql))
1125		elog(ERROR, "could not send query: %s", pchomp(PQerrorMessage(conn)));
1126
1127	if (!PQsetSingleRowMode(conn))	/* shouldn't fail */ view japanese
1128		elog(ERROR, "failed to set single-row mode for dblink query");
1129
1130	for (;;)
1131	{
1132		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
1133
1134		sinfo->cur_res = libpqsrv_get_result(conn, dblink_we_get_result);
1135		if (!sinfo->cur_res)
1136			break;
1137
1138		if (PQresultStatus(sinfo->cur_res) == PGRES_SINGLE_TUPLE)
1139		{
1140			/* got one row from possibly-bigger resultset */ view japanese
1141
1142			/*
1143			 * Set GUCs to ensure we read GUC-sensitive data types correctly.
1144			 * We shouldn't do this until we have a row in hand, to ensure
1145			 * libpq has seen any earlier ParameterStatus protocol messages.
1146			 */ view japanese
1147			if (first && nestlevel < 0)
1148				nestlevel = applyRemoteGucs(conn);
1149
1150			storeRow(sinfo, sinfo->cur_res, first);
1151
1152			PQclear(sinfo->cur_res);
1153			sinfo->cur_res = NULL;
1154			first = false;
1155		}
1156		else
1157		{
1158			/* if empty resultset, fill tuplestore header */ view japanese
1159			if (first && PQresultStatus(sinfo->cur_res) == PGRES_TUPLES_OK)
1160				storeRow(sinfo, sinfo->cur_res, first);
1161
1162			/* store completed result at last_res */ view japanese
1163			PQclear(sinfo->last_res);
1164			sinfo->last_res = sinfo->cur_res;
1165			sinfo->cur_res = NULL;
1166			first = true;
1167		}
1168	}
1169
1170	/* clean up GUC settings, if we changed any */ view japanese
1171	restoreLocalGucs(nestlevel);
1172
1173	/* return last_res */ view japanese
1174	res = sinfo->last_res;
1175	sinfo->last_res = NULL;
1176	return res;
1177}
1178
1179/*
1180 * Send single row to sinfo->tuplestore.
1181 *
1182 * If "first" is true, create the tuplestore using PGresult's metadata
1183 * (in this case the PGresult might contain either zero or one row).
1184 */ view japanese
1185static void
1186storeRow(volatile storeInfo *sinfo, PGresult *res, bool first)
1187{
1188	int			nfields = PQnfields(res);
1189	HeapTuple	tuple;
1190	int			i;
1191	MemoryContext oldcontext;
1192
1193	if (first)
1194	{
1195		/* Prepare for new result set */ view japanese
1196		ReturnSetInfo *rsinfo = (ReturnSetInfo *) sinfo->fcinfo->resultinfo;
1197		TupleDesc	tupdesc;
1198
1199		/*
1200		 * It's possible to get more than one result set if the query string
1201		 * contained multiple SQL commands.  In that case, we follow PQexec's
1202		 * traditional behavior of throwing away all but the last result.
1203		 */ view japanese
1204		if (sinfo->tuplestore)
1205			tuplestore_end(sinfo->tuplestore);
1206		sinfo->tuplestore = NULL;
1207
1208		/* get a tuple descriptor for our result type */ view japanese
1209		switch (get_call_result_type(sinfo->fcinfo, NULL, &tupdesc))
1210		{
1211			case TYPEFUNC_COMPOSITE:
1212				/* success */ view japanese
1213				break;
1214			case TYPEFUNC_RECORD:
1215				/* failed to determine actual type of RECORD */ view japanese
1216				ereport(ERROR,
1217						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1218						 errmsg("function returning record called in context "
1219								"that cannot accept type record")));
1220				break;
1221			default:
1222				/* result type isn't composite */ view japanese
1223				elog(ERROR, "return type must be a row type");
1224				break;
1225		}
1226
1227		/* make sure we have a persistent copy of the tupdesc */ view japanese
1228		tupdesc = CreateTupleDescCopy(tupdesc);
1229
1230		/* check result and tuple descriptor have the same number of columns */ view japanese
1231		if (nfields != tupdesc->natts)
1232			ereport(ERROR,
1233					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
1234					 errmsg("remote query result rowtype does not match "
1235							"the specified FROM clause rowtype")));
1236
1237		/* Prepare attinmeta for later data conversions */ view japanese
1238		sinfo->attinmeta = TupleDescGetAttInMetadata(tupdesc);
1239
1240		/* Create a new, empty tuplestore */ view japanese
1241		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(rsinfo->econtext->ecxt_per_query_memory);
1242		sinfo->tuplestore = tuplestore_begin_heap(true, false, work_mem);
1243		rsinfo->setResult = sinfo->tuplestore;
1244		rsinfo->setDesc = tupdesc;
1245		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
1246
1247		/* Done if empty resultset */ view japanese
1248		if (PQntuples(res) == 0)
1249			return;
1250
1251		/*
1252		 * Set up sufficiently-wide string pointers array; this won't change
1253		 * in size so it's easy to preallocate.
1254		 */ view japanese
1255		if (sinfo->cstrs)
1256			pfree(sinfo->cstrs);
1257		sinfo->cstrs = palloc_array(char *, nfields);
1258	}
1259
1260	/* Should have a single-row result if we get here */ view japanese
1261	Assert(PQntuples(res) == 1);
1262
1263	/*
1264	 * Do the following work in a temp context that we reset after each tuple.
1265	 * This cleans up not only the data we have direct access to, but any
1266	 * cruft the I/O functions might leak.
1267	 */ view japanese
1268	oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(sinfo->tmpcontext);
1269
1270	/*
1271	 * Fill cstrs with null-terminated strings of column values.
1272	 */ view japanese
1273	for (i = 0; i < nfields; i++)
1274	{
1275		if (PQgetisnull(res, 0, i))
1276			sinfo->cstrs[i] = NULL;
1277		else
1278			sinfo->cstrs[i] = PQgetvalue(res, 0, i);
1279	}
1280
1281	/* Convert row to a tuple, and add it to the tuplestore */ view japanese
1282	tuple = BuildTupleFromCStrings(sinfo->attinmeta, sinfo->cstrs);
1283
1284	tuplestore_puttuple(sinfo->tuplestore, tuple);
1285
1286	/* Clean up */ view japanese
1287	MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
1288	MemoryContextReset(sinfo->tmpcontext);
1289}
1290
1291/*
1292 * List all open dblink connections by name.
1293 * Returns an array of all connection names.
1294 * Takes no params
1295 */ view japanese
1296PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_get_connections);
1297Datum
1298dblink_get_connections(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1299{
1300	HASH_SEQ_STATUS status;
1301	remoteConnHashEnt *hentry;
1302	ArrayBuildState *astate = NULL;
1303
1304	if (remoteConnHash)
1305	{
1306		hash_seq_init(&status, remoteConnHash);
1307		while ((hentry = (remoteConnHashEnt *) hash_seq_search(&status)) != NULL)
1308		{
1309			/* ignore it if it's not an open connection */ view japanese
1310			if (hentry->rconn.conn == NULL)
1311				continue;
1312			/* stash away current value */ view japanese
1313			astate = accumArrayResult(astate,
1314									  CStringGetTextDatum(hentry->name),
1315									  false, TEXTOID, CurrentMemoryContext);
1316		}
1317	}
1318
1319	if (astate)
1320		PG_RETURN_DATUM(makeArrayResult(astate,
1321										CurrentMemoryContext));
1322	else
1323		PG_RETURN_NULL();
1324}
1325
1326/*
1327 * Checks if a given remote connection is busy
1328 *
1329 * Returns 1 if the connection is busy, 0 otherwise
1330 * Params:
1331 *	text connection_name - name of the connection to check
1332 *
1333 */ view japanese
1334PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_is_busy);
1335Datum
1336dblink_is_busy(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1337{
1338	PGconn	   *conn;
1339
1340	dblink_init();
1341	conn = dblink_get_named_conn(text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0)));
1342
1343	PQconsumeInput(conn);
1344	PG_RETURN_INT32(PQisBusy(conn));
1345}
1346
1347/*
1348 * Cancels a running request on a connection
1349 *
1350 * Returns text:
1351 *	"OK" if the cancel request has been sent correctly,
1352 *		an error message otherwise
1353 *
1354 * Params:
1355 *	text connection_name - name of the connection to check
1356 *
1357 */ view japanese
1358PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_cancel_query);
1359Datum
1360dblink_cancel_query(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1361{
1362	PGconn	   *conn;
1363	const char *msg;
1364	TimestampTz endtime;
1365
1366	dblink_init();
1367	conn = dblink_get_named_conn(text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0)));
1368	endtime = TimestampTzPlusMilliseconds(GetCurrentTimestamp(),
1369										  30000);
1370	msg = libpqsrv_cancel(conn, endtime);
1371	if (msg == NULL)
1372		msg = "OK";
1373
1374	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(msg));
1375}
1376
1377
1378/*
1379 * Get error message from a connection
1380 *
1381 * Returns text:
1382 *	"OK" if no error, an error message otherwise
1383 *
1384 * Params:
1385 *	text connection_name - name of the connection to check
1386 *
1387 */ view japanese
1388PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_error_message);
1389Datum
1390dblink_error_message(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1391{
1392	char	   *msg;
1393	PGconn	   *conn;
1394
1395	dblink_init();
1396	conn = dblink_get_named_conn(text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0)));
1397
1398	msg = PQerrorMessage(conn);
1399	if (msg == NULL || msg[0] == '\0')
1400		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text("OK"));
1401	else
1402		PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(pchomp(msg)));
1403}
1404
1405/*
1406 * Execute an SQL non-SELECT command
1407 */ view japanese
1408PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_exec);
1409Datum
1410dblink_exec(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1411{
1412	text	   *volatile sql_cmd_status = NULL;
1413	PGconn	   *volatile conn = NULL;
1414	volatile bool freeconn = false;
1415
1416	dblink_init();
1417
1418	PG_TRY();
1419	{
1420		PGresult   *res = NULL;
1421		char	   *sql = NULL;
1422		char	   *conname = NULL;
1423		bool		fail = true;	/* default to backward compatible behavior */ view japanese
1424
1425		if (PG_NARGS() == 3)
1426		{
1427			/* must be text,text,bool */ view japanese
1428			conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
1429			sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
1430			fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(2);
1431			dblink_get_conn(conname, &conn, &conname, &freeconn);
1432		}
1433		else if (PG_NARGS() == 2)
1434		{
1435			/* might be text,text or text,bool */ view japanese
1436			if (get_fn_expr_argtype(fcinfo->flinfo, 1) == BOOLOID)
1437			{
1438				sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
1439				fail = PG_GETARG_BOOL(1);
1440				conn = pconn->conn;
1441			}
1442			else
1443			{
1444				conname = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
1445				sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(1));
1446				dblink_get_conn(conname, &conn, &conname, &freeconn);
1447			}
1448		}
1449		else if (PG_NARGS() == 1)
1450		{
1451			/* must be single text argument */ view japanese
1452			conn = pconn->conn;
1453			sql = text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0));
1454		}
1455		else
1456			/* shouldn't happen */ view japanese
1457			elog(ERROR, "wrong number of arguments");
1458
1459		if (!conn)
1460			dblink_conn_not_avail(conname);
1461
1462		res = libpqsrv_exec(conn, sql, dblink_we_get_result);
1463		if (!res ||
1464			(PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK &&
1465			 PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_TUPLES_OK))
1466		{
1467			dblink_res_error(conn, conname, res, fail,
1468							 "while executing command");
1469
1470			/*
1471			 * and save a copy of the command status string to return as our
1472			 * result tuple
1473			 */
1474			sql_cmd_status = cstring_to_text("ERROR");
1475		}
1476		else if (PQresultStatus(res) == PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
1477		{
1478			/*
1479			 * and save a copy of the command status string to return as our
1480			 * result tuple
1481			 */ view japanese
1482			sql_cmd_status = cstring_to_text(PQcmdStatus(res));
1483			PQclear(res);
1484		}
1485		else
1486		{
1487			PQclear(res);
1488			ereport(ERROR,
1489					(errcode(ERRCODE_S_R_E_PROHIBITED_SQL_STATEMENT_ATTEMPTED),
1490					 errmsg("statement returning results not allowed")));
1491		}
1492	}
1493	PG_FINALLY();
1494	{
1495		/* if needed, close the connection to the database */ view japanese
1496		if (freeconn)
1497			libpqsrv_disconnect(conn);
1498	}
1499	PG_END_TRY();
1500
1501	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(sql_cmd_status);
1502}
1503
1504
1505/*
1506 * dblink_get_pkey
1507 *
1508 * Return list of primary key fields for the supplied relation,
1509 * or NULL if none exists.
1510 */ view japanese
1511PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_get_pkey);
1512Datum
1513dblink_get_pkey(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1514{
1515	int16		indnkeyatts;
1516	char	  **results;
1517	FuncCallContext *funcctx;
1518	int32		call_cntr;
1519	int32		max_calls;
1520	AttInMetadata *attinmeta;
1521	MemoryContext oldcontext;
1522
1523	/* stuff done only on the first call of the function */ view japanese
1524	if (SRF_IS_FIRSTCALL())
1525	{
1526		Relation	rel;
1527		TupleDesc	tupdesc;
1528
1529		/* create a function context for cross-call persistence */ view japanese
1530		funcctx = SRF_FIRSTCALL_INIT();
1531
1532		/*
1533		 * switch to memory context appropriate for multiple function calls
1534		 */ view japanese
1535		oldcontext = MemoryContextSwitchTo(funcctx->multi_call_memory_ctx);
1536
1537		/* open target relation */ view japanese
1538		rel = get_rel_from_relname(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0), AccessShareLock, ACL_SELECT);
1539
1540		/* get the array of attnums */ view japanese
1541		results = get_pkey_attnames(rel, &indnkeyatts);
1542
1543		relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
1544
1545		/*
1546		 * need a tuple descriptor representing one INT and one TEXT column
1547		 */ view japanese
1548		tupdesc = CreateTemplateTupleDesc(2);
1549		TupleDescInitEntry(tupdesc, (AttrNumber) 1, "position",
1550						   INT4OID, -1, 0);
1551		TupleDescInitEntry(tupdesc, (AttrNumber) 2, "colname",
1552						   TEXTOID, -1, 0);
1553
1554		/*
1555		 * Generate attribute metadata needed later to produce tuples from raw
1556		 * C strings
1557		 */ view japanese
1558		attinmeta = TupleDescGetAttInMetadata(tupdesc);
1559		funcctx->attinmeta = attinmeta;
1560
1561		if ((results != NULL) && (indnkeyatts > 0))
1562		{
1563			funcctx->max_calls = indnkeyatts;
1564
1565			/* got results, keep track of them */ view japanese
1566			funcctx->user_fctx = results;
1567		}
1568		else
1569		{
1570			/* fast track when no results */ view japanese
1571			MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
1572			SRF_RETURN_DONE(funcctx);
1573		}
1574
1575		MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcontext);
1576	}
1577
1578	/* stuff done on every call of the function */ view japanese
1579	funcctx = SRF_PERCALL_SETUP();
1580
1581	/*
1582	 * initialize per-call variables
1583	 */ view japanese
1584	call_cntr = funcctx->call_cntr;
1585	max_calls = funcctx->max_calls;
1586
1587	results = (char **) funcctx->user_fctx;
1588	attinmeta = funcctx->attinmeta;
1589
1590	if (call_cntr < max_calls)	/* do when there is more left to send */ view japanese
1591	{
1592		char	  **values;
1593		HeapTuple	tuple;
1594		Datum		result;
1595
1596		values = palloc_array(char *, 2);
1597		values[0] = psprintf("%d", call_cntr + 1);
1598		values[1] = results[call_cntr];
1599
1600		/* build the tuple */ view japanese
1601		tuple = BuildTupleFromCStrings(attinmeta, values);
1602
1603		/* make the tuple into a datum */ view japanese
1604		result = HeapTupleGetDatum(tuple);
1605
1606		SRF_RETURN_NEXT(funcctx, result);
1607	}
1608	else
1609	{
1610		/* do when there is no more left */ view japanese
1611		SRF_RETURN_DONE(funcctx);
1612	}
1613}
1614
1615
1616/*
1617 * dblink_build_sql_insert
1618 *
1619 * Used to generate an SQL insert statement
1620 * based on an existing tuple in a local relation.
1621 * This is useful for selectively replicating data
1622 * to another server via dblink.
1623 *
1624 * API:
1625 * <relname> - name of local table of interest
1626 * <pkattnums> - an int2vector of attnums which will be used
1627 * to identify the local tuple of interest
1628 * <pknumatts> - number of attnums in pkattnums
1629 * <src_pkattvals_arry> - text array of key values which will be used
1630 * to identify the local tuple of interest
1631 * <tgt_pkattvals_arry> - text array of key values which will be used
1632 * to build the string for execution remotely. These are substituted
1633 * for their counterparts in src_pkattvals_arry
1634 */ view japanese
1635PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_build_sql_insert);
1636Datum
1637dblink_build_sql_insert(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1638{
1639	text	   *relname_text = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1640	int2vector *pkattnums_arg = (int2vector *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(1);
1641	int32		pknumatts_arg = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
1642	ArrayType  *src_pkattvals_arry = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(3);
1643	ArrayType  *tgt_pkattvals_arry = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(4);
1644	Relation	rel;
1645	int		   *pkattnums;
1646	int			pknumatts;
1647	char	  **src_pkattvals;
1648	char	  **tgt_pkattvals;
1649	int			src_nitems;
1650	int			tgt_nitems;
1651	char	   *sql;
1652
1653	/*
1654	 * Open target relation.
1655	 */
1656	rel = get_rel_from_relname(relname_text, AccessShareLock, ACL_SELECT);
1657
1658	/*
1659	 * Process pkattnums argument.
1660	 */
1661	validate_pkattnums(rel, pkattnums_arg, pknumatts_arg,
1662					   &pkattnums, &pknumatts);
1663
1664	/*
1665	 * Source array is made up of key values that will be used to locate the
1666	 * tuple of interest from the local system.
1667	 */
1668	src_pkattvals = get_text_array_contents(src_pkattvals_arry, &src_nitems);
1669
1670	/*
1671	 * There should be one source array key value for each key attnum
1672	 */
1673	if (src_nitems != pknumatts)
1674		ereport(ERROR,
1675				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
1676				 errmsg("source key array length must match number of key attributes")));
1677
1678	/*
1679	 * Target array is made up of key values that will be used to build the
1680	 * SQL string for use on the remote system.
1681	 */
1682	tgt_pkattvals = get_text_array_contents(tgt_pkattvals_arry, &tgt_nitems);
1683
1684	/*
1685	 * There should be one target array key value for each key attnum
1686	 */
1687	if (tgt_nitems != pknumatts)
1688		ereport(ERROR,
1689				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
1690				 errmsg("target key array length must match number of key attributes")));
1691
1692	/*
1693	 * Prep work is finally done. Go get the SQL string.
1694	 */
1695	sql = get_sql_insert(rel, pkattnums, pknumatts, src_pkattvals, tgt_pkattvals);
1696
1697	/*
1698	 * Now we can close the relation.
1699	 */
1700	relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
1701
1702	/*
1703	 * And send it
1704	 */
1705	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(sql));
1706}
1707
1708
1709/*
1710 * dblink_build_sql_delete
1711 *
1712 * Used to generate an SQL delete statement.
1713 * This is useful for selectively replicating a
1714 * delete to another server via dblink.
1715 *
1716 * API:
1717 * <relname> - name of remote table of interest
1718 * <pkattnums> - an int2vector of attnums which will be used
1719 * to identify the remote tuple of interest
1720 * <pknumatts> - number of attnums in pkattnums
1721 * <tgt_pkattvals_arry> - text array of key values which will be used
1722 * to build the string for execution remotely.
1723 */ view japanese
1724PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_build_sql_delete);
1725Datum
1726dblink_build_sql_delete(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1727{
1728	text	   *relname_text = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1729	int2vector *pkattnums_arg = (int2vector *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(1);
1730	int32		pknumatts_arg = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
1731	ArrayType  *tgt_pkattvals_arry = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(3);
1732	Relation	rel;
1733	int		   *pkattnums;
1734	int			pknumatts;
1735	char	  **tgt_pkattvals;
1736	int			tgt_nitems;
1737	char	   *sql;
1738
1739	/*
1740	 * Open target relation.
1741	 */
1742	rel = get_rel_from_relname(relname_text, AccessShareLock, ACL_SELECT);
1743
1744	/*
1745	 * Process pkattnums argument.
1746	 */
1747	validate_pkattnums(rel, pkattnums_arg, pknumatts_arg,
1748					   &pkattnums, &pknumatts);
1749
1750	/*
1751	 * Target array is made up of key values that will be used to build the
1752	 * SQL string for use on the remote system.
1753	 */
1754	tgt_pkattvals = get_text_array_contents(tgt_pkattvals_arry, &tgt_nitems);
1755
1756	/*
1757	 * There should be one target array key value for each key attnum
1758	 */
1759	if (tgt_nitems != pknumatts)
1760		ereport(ERROR,
1761				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
1762				 errmsg("target key array length must match number of key attributes")));
1763
1764	/*
1765	 * Prep work is finally done. Go get the SQL string.
1766	 */
1767	sql = get_sql_delete(rel, pkattnums, pknumatts, tgt_pkattvals);
1768
1769	/*
1770	 * Now we can close the relation.
1771	 */
1772	relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
1773
1774	/*
1775	 * And send it
1776	 */
1777	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(sql));
1778}
1779
1780
1781/*
1782 * dblink_build_sql_update
1783 *
1784 * Used to generate an SQL update statement
1785 * based on an existing tuple in a local relation.
1786 * This is useful for selectively replicating data
1787 * to another server via dblink.
1788 *
1789 * API:
1790 * <relname> - name of local table of interest
1791 * <pkattnums> - an int2vector of attnums which will be used
1792 * to identify the local tuple of interest
1793 * <pknumatts> - number of attnums in pkattnums
1794 * <src_pkattvals_arry> - text array of key values which will be used
1795 * to identify the local tuple of interest
1796 * <tgt_pkattvals_arry> - text array of key values which will be used
1797 * to build the string for execution remotely. These are substituted
1798 * for their counterparts in src_pkattvals_arry
1799 */ view japanese
1800PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_build_sql_update);
1801Datum
1802dblink_build_sql_update(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1803{
1804	text	   *relname_text = PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0);
1805	int2vector *pkattnums_arg = (int2vector *) PG_GETARG_POINTER(1);
1806	int32		pknumatts_arg = PG_GETARG_INT32(2);
1807	ArrayType  *src_pkattvals_arry = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(3);
1808	ArrayType  *tgt_pkattvals_arry = PG_GETARG_ARRAYTYPE_P(4);
1809	Relation	rel;
1810	int		   *pkattnums;
1811	int			pknumatts;
1812	char	  **src_pkattvals;
1813	char	  **tgt_pkattvals;
1814	int			src_nitems;
1815	int			tgt_nitems;
1816	char	   *sql;
1817
1818	/*
1819	 * Open target relation.
1820	 */ view japanese
1821	rel = get_rel_from_relname(relname_text, AccessShareLock, ACL_SELECT);
1822
1823	/*
1824	 * Process pkattnums argument.
1825	 */ view japanese
1826	validate_pkattnums(rel, pkattnums_arg, pknumatts_arg,
1827					   &pkattnums, &pknumatts);
1828
1829	/*
1830	 * Source array is made up of key values that will be used to locate the
1831	 * tuple of interest from the local system.
1832	 */ view japanese
1833	src_pkattvals = get_text_array_contents(src_pkattvals_arry, &src_nitems);
1834
1835	/*
1836	 * There should be one source array key value for each key attnum
1837	 */ view japanese
1838	if (src_nitems != pknumatts)
1839		ereport(ERROR,
1840				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
1841				 errmsg("source key array length must match number of key attributes")));
1842
1843	/*
1844	 * Target array is made up of key values that will be used to build the
1845	 * SQL string for use on the remote system.
1846	 */ view japanese
1847	tgt_pkattvals = get_text_array_contents(tgt_pkattvals_arry, &tgt_nitems);
1848
1849	/*
1850	 * There should be one target array key value for each key attnum
1851	 */ view japanese
1852	if (tgt_nitems != pknumatts)
1853		ereport(ERROR,
1854				(errcode(ERRCODE_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_ERROR),
1855				 errmsg("target key array length must match number of key attributes")));
1856
1857	/*
1858	 * Prep work is finally done. Go get the SQL string.
1859	 */ view japanese
1860	sql = get_sql_update(rel, pkattnums, pknumatts, src_pkattvals, tgt_pkattvals);
1861
1862	/*
1863	 * Now we can close the relation.
1864	 */ view japanese
1865	relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);
1866
1867	/*
1868	 * And send it
1869	 */ view japanese
1870	PG_RETURN_TEXT_P(cstring_to_text(sql));
1871}
1872
1873/*
1874 * dblink_current_query
1875 * return the current query string
1876 * to allow its use in (among other things)
1877 * rewrite rules
1878 */ view japanese
1879PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_current_query);
1880Datum
1881dblink_current_query(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1882{
1883	/* This is now just an alias for the built-in function current_query() */ view japanese
1884	PG_RETURN_DATUM(current_query(fcinfo));
1885}
1886
1887/*
1888 * Retrieve async notifications for a connection.
1889 *
1890 * Returns a setof record of notifications, or an empty set if none received.
1891 * Can optionally take a named connection as parameter, but uses the unnamed
1892 * connection per default.
1893 *
1894 */ view japanese
1895#define DBLINK_NOTIFY_COLS		3
1896
1897PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_get_notify);
1898Datum
1899dblink_get_notify(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1900{
1901	PGconn	   *conn;
1902	PGnotify   *notify;
1903	ReturnSetInfo *rsinfo = (ReturnSetInfo *) fcinfo->resultinfo;
1904
1905	dblink_init();
1906	if (PG_NARGS() == 1)
1907		conn = dblink_get_named_conn(text_to_cstring(PG_GETARG_TEXT_PP(0)));
1908	else
1909		conn = pconn->conn;
1910
1911	InitMaterializedSRF(fcinfo, 0);
1912
1913	PQconsumeInput(conn);
1914	while ((notify = PQnotifies(conn)) != NULL)
1915	{
1916		Datum		values[DBLINK_NOTIFY_COLS];
1917		bool		nulls[DBLINK_NOTIFY_COLS];
1918
1919		memset(values, 0, sizeof(values));
1920		memset(nulls, 0, sizeof(nulls));
1921
1922		if (notify->relname != NULL)
1923			values[0] = CStringGetTextDatum(notify->relname);
1924		else
1925			nulls[0] = true;
1926
1927		values[1] = Int32GetDatum(notify->be_pid);
1928
1929		if (notify->extra != NULL)
1930			values[2] = CStringGetTextDatum(notify->extra);
1931		else
1932			nulls[2] = true;
1933
1934		tuplestore_putvalues(rsinfo->setResult, rsinfo->setDesc, values, nulls);
1935
1936		PQfreemem(notify);
1937		PQconsumeInput(conn);
1938	}
1939
1940	return (Datum) 0;
1941}
1942
1943/*
1944 * Validate the options given to a dblink foreign server or user mapping.
1945 * Raise an error if any option is invalid.
1946 *
1947 * We just check the names of options here, so semantic errors in options,
1948 * such as invalid numeric format, will be detected at the attempt to connect.
1949 */ view japanese
1950PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(dblink_fdw_validator);
1951Datum
1952dblink_fdw_validator(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1953{
1954	List	   *options_list = untransformRelOptions(PG_GETARG_DATUM(0));
1955	Oid			context = PG_GETARG_OID(1);
1956	ListCell   *cell;
1957
1958	static const PQconninfoOption *options = NULL;
1959
1960	/*
1961	 * Get list of valid libpq options.
1962	 *
1963	 * To avoid unnecessary work, we get the list once and use it throughout
1964	 * the lifetime of this backend process.  We don't need to care about
1965	 * memory context issues, because PQconndefaults allocates with malloc.
1966	 */
1967	if (!options)
1968	{
1969		options = PQconndefaults();
1970		if (!options)			/* assume reason for failure is OOM */
1971			ereport(ERROR,
1972					(errcode(ERRCODE_FDW_OUT_OF_MEMORY),
1973					 errmsg("out of memory"),
1974					 errdetail("Could not get libpq's default connection options.")));
1975	}
1976
1977	/* Validate each supplied option. */ view japanese
1978	foreach(cell, options_list)
1979	{
1980		DefElem    *def = (DefElem *) lfirst(cell);
1981
1982		if (!is_valid_dblink_fdw_option(options, def->defname, context))
1983		{
1984			/*
1985			 * Unknown option, or invalid option for the context specified, so
1986			 * complain about it.  Provide a hint with a valid option that
1987			 * looks similar, if there is one.
1988			 */ view japanese
1989			const PQconninfoOption *opt;
1990			const char *closest_match;
1991			ClosestMatchState match_state;
1992			bool		has_valid_options = false;
1993
1994			initClosestMatch(&match_state, def->defname, 4);
1995			for (opt = options; opt->keyword; opt++)
1996			{
1997				if (is_valid_dblink_option(options, opt->keyword, context))
1998				{
1999					has_valid_options = true;
2000					updateClosestMatch(&match_state, opt->keyword);
2001				}
2002			}
2003
2004			closest_match = getClosestMatch(&match_state);
2005			ereport(ERROR,
2006					(errcode(ERRCODE_FDW_OPTION_NAME_NOT_FOUND),
2007					 errmsg("invalid option \"%s\"", def->defname),
2008					 has_valid_options ? closest_match ?
2009					 errhint("Perhaps you meant the option \"%s\".",
2010							 closest_match) : 0 :
2011					 errhint("There are no valid options in this context.")));
2012		}
2013	}
2014
2015	PG_RETURN_VOID();
2016}
2017
2018
2019/*************************************************************
2020 * internal functions
2021 */ view japanese
2022
2023
2024/*
2025 * get_pkey_attnames
2026 *
2027 * Get the primary key attnames for the given relation.
2028 * Return NULL, and set indnkeyatts = 0, if no primary key exists.
2029 */ view japanese
2030static char **
2031get_pkey_attnames(Relation rel, int16 *indnkeyatts)
2032{
2033	Relation	indexRelation;
2034	ScanKeyData skey;
2035	SysScanDesc scan;
2036	HeapTuple	indexTuple;
2037	int			i;
2038	char	  **result = NULL;
2039	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
2040
2041	/* initialize indnkeyatts to 0 in case no primary key exists */ view japanese
2042	*indnkeyatts = 0;
2043
2044	tupdesc = rel->rd_att;
2045
2046	/* Prepare to scan pg_index for entries having indrelid = this rel. */ view japanese
2047	indexRelation = table_open(IndexRelationId, AccessShareLock);
2048	ScanKeyInit(&skey,
2049				Anum_pg_index_indrelid,
2050				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
2051				ObjectIdGetDatum(RelationGetRelid(rel)));
2052
2053	scan = systable_beginscan(indexRelation, IndexIndrelidIndexId, true,
2054							  NULL, 1, &skey);
2055
2056	while (HeapTupleIsValid(indexTuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
2057	{
2058		Form_pg_index index = (Form_pg_index) GETSTRUCT(indexTuple);
2059
2060		/* we're only interested if it is the primary key */ view japanese
2061		if (index->indisprimary)
2062		{
2063			*indnkeyatts = index->indnkeyatts;
2064			if (*indnkeyatts > 0)
2065			{
2066				result = palloc_array(char *, *indnkeyatts);
2067
2068				for (i = 0; i < *indnkeyatts; i++)
2069					result[i] = SPI_fname(tupdesc, index->indkey.values[i]);
2070			}
2071			break;
2072		}
2073	}
2074
2075	systable_endscan(scan);
2076	table_close(indexRelation, AccessShareLock);
2077
2078	return result;
2079}
2080
2081/*
2082 * Deconstruct a text[] into C-strings (note any NULL elements will be
2083 * returned as NULL pointers)
2084 */ view japanese
2085static char **
2086get_text_array_contents(ArrayType *array, int *numitems)
2087{
2088	int			ndim = ARR_NDIM(array);
2089	int		   *dims = ARR_DIMS(array);
2090	int			nitems;
2091	int16		typlen;
2092	bool		typbyval;
2093	char		typalign;
2094	char	  **values;
2095	char	   *ptr;
2096	bits8	   *bitmap;
2097	int			bitmask;
2098	int			i;
2099
2100	Assert(ARR_ELEMTYPE(array) == TEXTOID);
2101
2102	*numitems = nitems = ArrayGetNItems(ndim, dims);
2103
2104	get_typlenbyvalalign(ARR_ELEMTYPE(array),
2105						 &typlen, &typbyval, &typalign);
2106
2107	values = palloc_array(char *, nitems);
2108
2109	ptr = ARR_DATA_PTR(array);
2110	bitmap = ARR_NULLBITMAP(array);
2111	bitmask = 1;
2112
2113	for (i = 0; i < nitems; i++)
2114	{
2115		if (bitmap && (*bitmap & bitmask) == 0)
2116		{
2117			values[i] = NULL;
2118		}
2119		else
2120		{
2121			values[i] = TextDatumGetCString(PointerGetDatum(ptr));
2122			ptr = att_addlength_pointer(ptr, typlen, ptr);
2123			ptr = (char *) att_align_nominal(ptr, typalign);
2124		}
2125
2126		/* advance bitmap pointer if any */ view japanese
2127		if (bitmap)
2128		{
2129			bitmask <<= 1;
2130			if (bitmask == 0x100)
2131			{
2132				bitmap++;
2133				bitmask = 1;
2134			}
2135		}
2136	}
2137
2138	return values;
2139}
2140
2141static char *
2142get_sql_insert(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **src_pkattvals, char **tgt_pkattvals)
2143{
2144	char	   *relname;
2145	HeapTuple	tuple;
2146	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
2147	int			natts;
2148	StringInfoData buf;
2149	char	   *val;
2150	int			key;
2151	int			i;
2152	bool		needComma;
2153
2154	initStringInfo(&buf);
2155
2156	/* get relation name including any needed schema prefix and quoting */
2157	relname = generate_relation_name(rel);
2158
2159	tupdesc = rel->rd_att;
2160	natts = tupdesc->natts;
2161
2162	tuple = get_tuple_of_interest(rel, pkattnums, pknumatts, src_pkattvals);
2163	if (!tuple)
2164		ereport(ERROR,
2165				(errcode(ERRCODE_CARDINALITY_VIOLATION),
2166				 errmsg("source row not found")));
2167
2168	appendStringInfo(&buf, "INSERT INTO %s(", relname);
2169
2170	needComma = false;
2171	for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
2172	{
2173		Form_pg_attribute att = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i);
2174
2175		if (att->attisdropped)
2176			continue;
2177
2178		if (needComma)
2179			appendStringInfoChar(&buf, ',');
2180
2181		appendStringInfoString(&buf,
2182							   quote_ident_cstr(NameStr(att->attname)));
2183		needComma = true;
2184	}
2185
2186	appendStringInfoString(&buf, ") VALUES(");
2187
2188	/*
2189	 * Note: i is physical column number (counting from 0).
2190	 */
2191	needComma = false;
2192	for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
2193	{
2194		if (TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i)->attisdropped)
2195			continue;
2196
2197		if (needComma)
2198			appendStringInfoChar(&buf, ',');
2199
2200		key = get_attnum_pk_pos(pkattnums, pknumatts, i);
2201
2202		if (key >= 0)
2203			val = tgt_pkattvals[key] ? pstrdup(tgt_pkattvals[key]) : NULL;
2204		else
2205			val = SPI_getvalue(tuple, tupdesc, i + 1);
2206
2207		if (val != NULL)
2208		{
2209			appendStringInfoString(&buf, quote_literal_cstr(val));
2210			pfree(val);
2211		}
2212		else
2213			appendStringInfoString(&buf, "NULL");
2214		needComma = true;
2215	}
2216	appendStringInfoChar(&buf, ')');
2217
2218	return buf.data;
2219}
2220
2221static char *
2222get_sql_delete(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **tgt_pkattvals)
2223{
2224	char	   *relname;
2225	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
2226	StringInfoData buf;
2227	int			i;
2228
2229	initStringInfo(&buf);
2230
2231	/* get relation name including any needed schema prefix and quoting */
2232	relname = generate_relation_name(rel);
2233
2234	tupdesc = rel->rd_att;
2235
2236	appendStringInfo(&buf, "DELETE FROM %s WHERE ", relname);
2237	for (i = 0; i < pknumatts; i++)
2238	{
2239		int			pkattnum = pkattnums[i];
2240		Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, pkattnum);
2241
2242		if (i > 0)
2243			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " AND ");
2244
2245		appendStringInfoString(&buf,
2246							   quote_ident_cstr(NameStr(attr->attname)));
2247
2248		if (tgt_pkattvals[i] != NULL)
2249			appendStringInfo(&buf, " = %s",
2250							 quote_literal_cstr(tgt_pkattvals[i]));
2251		else
2252			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " IS NULL");
2253	}
2254
2255	return buf.data;
2256}
2257
2258static char *
2259get_sql_update(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **src_pkattvals, char **tgt_pkattvals)
2260{
2261	char	   *relname;
2262	HeapTuple	tuple;
2263	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
2264	int			natts;
2265	StringInfoData buf;
2266	char	   *val;
2267	int			key;
2268	int			i;
2269	bool		needComma;
2270
2271	initStringInfo(&buf);
2272
2273	/* get relation name including any needed schema prefix and quoting */
2274	relname = generate_relation_name(rel);
2275
2276	tupdesc = rel->rd_att;
2277	natts = tupdesc->natts;
2278
2279	tuple = get_tuple_of_interest(rel, pkattnums, pknumatts, src_pkattvals);
2280	if (!tuple)
2281		ereport(ERROR,
2282				(errcode(ERRCODE_CARDINALITY_VIOLATION),
2283				 errmsg("source row not found")));
2284
2285	appendStringInfo(&buf, "UPDATE %s SET ", relname);
2286
2287	/*
2288	 * Note: i is physical column number (counting from 0).
2289	 */ view japanese
2290	needComma = false;
2291	for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
2292	{
2293		Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i);
2294
2295		if (attr->attisdropped)
2296			continue;
2297
2298		if (needComma)
2299			appendStringInfoString(&buf, ", ");
2300
2301		appendStringInfo(&buf, "%s = ",
2302						 quote_ident_cstr(NameStr(attr->attname)));
2303
2304		key = get_attnum_pk_pos(pkattnums, pknumatts, i);
2305
2306		if (key >= 0)
2307			val = tgt_pkattvals[key] ? pstrdup(tgt_pkattvals[key]) : NULL;
2308		else
2309			val = SPI_getvalue(tuple, tupdesc, i + 1);
2310
2311		if (val != NULL)
2312		{
2313			appendStringInfoString(&buf, quote_literal_cstr(val));
2314			pfree(val);
2315		}
2316		else
2317			appendStringInfoString(&buf, "NULL");
2318		needComma = true;
2319	}
2320
2321	appendStringInfoString(&buf, " WHERE ");
2322
2323	for (i = 0; i < pknumatts; i++)
2324	{
2325		int			pkattnum = pkattnums[i];
2326		Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, pkattnum);
2327
2328		if (i > 0)
2329			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " AND ");
2330
2331		appendStringInfoString(&buf,
2332							   quote_ident_cstr(NameStr(attr->attname)));
2333
2334		val = tgt_pkattvals[i];
2335
2336		if (val != NULL)
2337			appendStringInfo(&buf, " = %s", quote_literal_cstr(val));
2338		else
2339			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " IS NULL");
2340	}
2341
2342	return buf.data;
2343}
2344
2345/*
2346 * Return a properly quoted identifier.
2347 * Uses quote_ident in quote.c
2348 */ view japanese
2349static char *
2350quote_ident_cstr(char *rawstr)
2351{
2352	text	   *rawstr_text;
2353	text	   *result_text;
2354	char	   *result;
2355
2356	rawstr_text = cstring_to_text(rawstr);
2357	result_text = DatumGetTextPP(DirectFunctionCall1(quote_ident,
2358													 PointerGetDatum(rawstr_text)));
2359	result = text_to_cstring(result_text);
2360
2361	return result;
2362}
2363
2364static int
2365get_attnum_pk_pos(int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, int key)
2366{
2367	int			i;
2368
2369	/*
2370	 * Not likely a long list anyway, so just scan for the value
2371	 */ view japanese
2372	for (i = 0; i < pknumatts; i++)
2373		if (key == pkattnums[i])
2374			return i;
2375
2376	return -1;
2377}
2378
2379static HeapTuple
2380get_tuple_of_interest(Relation rel, int *pkattnums, int pknumatts, char **src_pkattvals)
2381{
2382	char	   *relname;
2383	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
2384	int			natts;
2385	StringInfoData buf;
2386	int			ret;
2387	HeapTuple	tuple;
2388	int			i;
2389
2390	/*
2391	 * Connect to SPI manager
2392	 */ view japanese
2393	SPI_connect();
2394
2395	initStringInfo(&buf);
2396
2397	/* get relation name including any needed schema prefix and quoting */ view japanese
2398	relname = generate_relation_name(rel);
2399
2400	tupdesc = rel->rd_att;
2401	natts = tupdesc->natts;
2402
2403	/*
2404	 * Build sql statement to look up tuple of interest, ie, the one matching
2405	 * src_pkattvals.  We used to use "SELECT *" here, but it's simpler to
2406	 * generate a result tuple that matches the table's physical structure,
2407	 * with NULLs for any dropped columns.  Otherwise we have to deal with two
2408	 * different tupdescs and everything's very confusing.
2409	 */ view japanese
2410	appendStringInfoString(&buf, "SELECT ");
2411
2412	for (i = 0; i < natts; i++)
2413	{
2414		Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, i);
2415
2416		if (i > 0)
2417			appendStringInfoString(&buf, ", ");
2418
2419		if (attr->attisdropped)
2420			appendStringInfoString(&buf, "NULL");
2421		else
2422			appendStringInfoString(&buf,
2423								   quote_ident_cstr(NameStr(attr->attname)));
2424	}
2425
2426	appendStringInfo(&buf, " FROM %s WHERE ", relname);
2427
2428	for (i = 0; i < pknumatts; i++)
2429	{
2430		int			pkattnum = pkattnums[i];
2431		Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, pkattnum);
2432
2433		if (i > 0)
2434			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " AND ");
2435
2436		appendStringInfoString(&buf,
2437							   quote_ident_cstr(NameStr(attr->attname)));
2438
2439		if (src_pkattvals[i] != NULL)
2440			appendStringInfo(&buf, " = %s",
2441							 quote_literal_cstr(src_pkattvals[i]));
2442		else
2443			appendStringInfoString(&buf, " IS NULL");
2444	}
2445
2446	/*
2447	 * Retrieve the desired tuple
2448	 */ view japanese
2449	ret = SPI_exec(buf.data, 0);
2450	pfree(buf.data);
2451
2452	/*
2453	 * Only allow one qualifying tuple
2454	 */ view japanese
2455	if ((ret == SPI_OK_SELECT) && (SPI_processed > 1))
2456		ereport(ERROR,
2457				(errcode(ERRCODE_CARDINALITY_VIOLATION),
2458				 errmsg("source criteria matched more than one record")));
2459
2460	else if (ret == SPI_OK_SELECT && SPI_processed == 1)
2461	{
2462		SPITupleTable *tuptable = SPI_tuptable;
2463
2464		tuple = SPI_copytuple(tuptable->vals[0]);
2465		SPI_finish();
2466
2467		return tuple;
2468	}
2469	else
2470	{
2471		/*
2472		 * no qualifying tuples
2473		 */ view japanese
2474		SPI_finish();
2475
2476		return NULL;
2477	}
2478
2479	/*
2480	 * never reached, but keep compiler quiet
2481	 */ view japanese
2482	return NULL;
2483}
2484
2485/*
2486 * Open the relation named by relname_text, acquire specified type of lock,
2487 * verify we have specified permissions.
2488 * Caller must close rel when done with it.
2489 */ view japanese
2490static Relation
2491get_rel_from_relname(text *relname_text, LOCKMODE lockmode, AclMode aclmode)
2492{
2493	RangeVar   *relvar;
2494	Relation	rel;
2495	AclResult	aclresult;
2496
2497	relvar = makeRangeVarFromNameList(textToQualifiedNameList(relname_text));
2498	rel = table_openrv(relvar, lockmode);
2499
2500	aclresult = pg_class_aclcheck(RelationGetRelid(rel), GetUserId(),
2501								  aclmode);
2502	if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
2503		aclcheck_error(aclresult, get_relkind_objtype(rel->rd_rel->relkind),
2504					   RelationGetRelationName(rel));
2505
2506	return rel;
2507}
2508
2509/*
2510 * generate_relation_name - copied from ruleutils.c
2511 *		Compute the name to display for a relation
2512 *
2513 * The result includes all necessary quoting and schema-prefixing.
2514 */ view japanese
2515static char *
2516generate_relation_name(Relation rel)
2517{
2518	char	   *nspname;
2519	char	   *result;
2520
2521	/* Qualify the name if not visible in search path */ view japanese
2522	if (RelationIsVisible(RelationGetRelid(rel)))
2523		nspname = NULL;
2524	else
2525		nspname = get_namespace_name(rel->rd_rel->relnamespace);
2526
2527	result = quote_qualified_identifier(nspname, RelationGetRelationName(rel));
2528
2529	return result;
2530}
2531
2532
2533static remoteConn *
2534getConnectionByName(const char *name)
2535{
2536	remoteConnHashEnt *hentry;
2537	char	   *key;
2538
2539	if (!remoteConnHash)
2540		remoteConnHash = createConnHash();
2541
2542	key = pstrdup(name);
2543	truncate_identifier(key, strlen(key), false);
2544	hentry = (remoteConnHashEnt *) hash_search(remoteConnHash,
2545											   key, HASH_FIND, NULL);
2546
2547	if (hentry && hentry->rconn.conn != NULL)
2548		return &hentry->rconn;
2549
2550	return NULL;
2551}
2552
2553static HTAB *
2554createConnHash(void)
2555{
2556	HASHCTL		ctl;
2557
2558	ctl.keysize = NAMEDATALEN;
2559	ctl.entrysize = sizeof(remoteConnHashEnt);
2560
2561	return hash_create("Remote Con hash", NUMCONN, &ctl,
2562					   HASH_ELEM | HASH_STRINGS);
2563}
2564
2565static remoteConn *
2566createNewConnection(const char *name)
2567{
2568	remoteConnHashEnt *hentry;
2569	bool		found;
2570	char	   *key;
2571
2572	if (!remoteConnHash)
2573		remoteConnHash = createConnHash();
2574
2575	key = pstrdup(name);
2576	truncate_identifier(key, strlen(key), true);
2577	hentry = (remoteConnHashEnt *) hash_search(remoteConnHash, key,
2578											   HASH_ENTER, &found);
2579
2580	if (found && hentry->rconn.conn != NULL)
2581		ereport(ERROR,
2582				(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
2583				 errmsg("duplicate connection name")));
2584
2585	/* New, or reusable, so initialize the rconn struct to zeroes */ view japanese
2586	memset(&hentry->rconn, 0, sizeof(remoteConn));
2587
2588	return &hentry->rconn;
2589}
2590
2591static void
2592deleteConnection(const char *name)
2593{
2594	remoteConnHashEnt *hentry;
2595	bool		found;
2596	char	   *key;
2597
2598	if (!remoteConnHash)
2599		remoteConnHash = createConnHash();
2600
2601	key = pstrdup(name);
2602	truncate_identifier(key, strlen(key), false);
2603	hentry = (remoteConnHashEnt *) hash_search(remoteConnHash,
2604											   key, HASH_REMOVE, &found);
2605
2606	if (!hentry)
2607		ereport(ERROR,
2608				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
2609				 errmsg("undefined connection name")));
2610}
2611
2612 /*
2613  * Ensure that require_auth and SCRAM keys are correctly set on connstr.
2614  * SCRAM keys used to pass-through are coming from the initial connection
2615  * from the client with the server.
2616  *
2617  * All required SCRAM options are set by dblink, so we just need to ensure
2618  * that these options are not overwritten by the user.
2619  *
2620  * See appendSCRAMKeysInfo and its usage for more.
2621  */ view japanese
2622bool
2623dblink_connstr_has_required_scram_options(const char *connstr)
2624{
2625	PQconninfoOption *options;
2626	bool		has_scram_server_key = false;
2627	bool		has_scram_client_key = false;
2628	bool		has_require_auth = false;
2629	bool		has_scram_keys = false;
2630
2631	options = PQconninfoParse(connstr, NULL);
2632	if (options)
2633	{
2634		/*
2635		 * Continue iterating even if we found the keys that we need to
2636		 * validate to make sure that there is no other declaration of these
2637		 * keys that can overwrite the first.
2638		 */ view japanese
2639		for (PQconninfoOption *option = options; option->keyword != NULL; option++)
2640		{
2641			if (strcmp(option->keyword, "require_auth") == 0)
2642			{
2643				if (option->val != NULL && strcmp(option->val, "scram-sha-256") == 0)
2644					has_require_auth = true;
2645				else
2646					has_require_auth = false;
2647			}
2648
2649			if (strcmp(option->keyword, "scram_client_key") == 0)
2650			{
2651				if (option->val != NULL && option->val[0] != '\0')
2652					has_scram_client_key = true;
2653				else
2654					has_scram_client_key = false;
2655			}
2656
2657			if (strcmp(option->keyword, "scram_server_key") == 0)
2658			{
2659				if (option->val != NULL && option->val[0] != '\0')
2660					has_scram_server_key = true;
2661				else
2662					has_scram_server_key = false;
2663			}
2664		}
2665		PQconninfoFree(options);
2666	}
2667
2668	has_scram_keys = has_scram_client_key && has_scram_server_key && MyProcPort != NULL && MyProcPort->has_scram_keys;
2669
2670	return (has_scram_keys && has_require_auth);
2671}
2672
2673/*
2674 * We need to make sure that the connection made used credentials
2675 * which were provided by the user, so check what credentials were
2676 * used to connect and then make sure that they came from the user.
2677 *
2678 * On failure, we close "conn" and also delete the hashtable entry
2679 * identified by "connname" (if that's not NULL).
2680 */ view japanese
2681static void
2682dblink_security_check(PGconn *conn, const char *connname, const char *connstr)
2683{
2684	/* Superuser bypasses security check */ view japanese
2685	if (superuser())
2686		return;
2687
2688	/* If password was used to connect, make sure it was one provided */ view japanese
2689	if (PQconnectionUsedPassword(conn) && dblink_connstr_has_pw(connstr))
2690		return;
2691
2692	/*
2693	 * Password was not used to connect, check if SCRAM pass-through is in
2694	 * use.
2695	 *
2696	 * If dblink_connstr_has_required_scram_options is true we assume that
2697	 * UseScramPassthrough is also true because the required SCRAM keys are
2698	 * only added if UseScramPassthrough is set, and the user is not allowed
2699	 * to add the SCRAM keys on fdw and user mapping options.
2700	 */ view japanese
2701	if (MyProcPort != NULL && MyProcPort->has_scram_keys && dblink_connstr_has_required_scram_options(connstr))
2702		return;
2703
2704#ifdef ENABLE_GSS
2705	/* If GSSAPI creds used to connect, make sure it was one delegated */ view japanese
2706	if (PQconnectionUsedGSSAPI(conn) && be_gssapi_get_delegation(MyProcPort))
2707		return;
2708#endif
2709
2710	/* Otherwise, fail out */ view japanese
2711	libpqsrv_disconnect(conn);
2712	if (connname)
2713		deleteConnection(connname);
2714
2715	ereport(ERROR,
2716			(errcode(ERRCODE_S_R_E_PROHIBITED_SQL_STATEMENT_ATTEMPTED),
2717			 errmsg("password or GSSAPI delegated credentials required"),
2718			 errdetail("Non-superusers may only connect using credentials they provide, eg: password in connection string or delegated GSSAPI credentials"),
2719			 errhint("Ensure provided credentials match target server's authentication method.")));
2720}
2721
2722/*
2723 * Function to check if the connection string includes an explicit
2724 * password, needed to ensure that non-superuser password-based auth
2725 * is using a provided password and not one picked up from the
2726 * environment.
2727 */ view japanese
2728static bool
2729dblink_connstr_has_pw(const char *connstr)
2730{
2731	PQconninfoOption *options;
2732	PQconninfoOption *option;
2733	bool		connstr_gives_password = false;
2734
2735	options = PQconninfoParse(connstr, NULL);
